description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a slightly bulging cylindrical shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves held together by three wide metal hoops. The wood has a natural brown finish with visible grain patterns, giving it a rustic and sturdy appearance. The metal bands are matte gray and wrap horizontally around the top, middle, and bottom of the barrel, fastened with round rivets. The top surface is flat and made of the same wood, with a small bunghole visible near the edge, suggesting it can be used for storing or transporting liquids or dry goods. The overall craftsmanship suggests durability and traditional cooperage techniques. There are no visible handles, labels, or decorative markings, and the barrel appears well-sealed.
description: A traditional wooden barrel with three gray metal hoops, brown wood staves, and a flat wooden top with a small bunghole. The surface is rustic and sturdy, with no handles or markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the barrel's bulging shape, three metal hoops, and a small bunghole near the top edge.
description_view_1: Side view with the bunghole more centered, displaying the wood grain and all three metal hoops clearly.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the bunghole visible at the top left, emphasizing the smooth curvature and metal bands.
description_view_3: Rear view with the bunghole at the top right, showing the continuous wood grain and metal reinforcement.
